description_long: This is a hand tool with a contoured, dual-tone handle in red and tan, designed for ergonomic grip and comfort. The handle appears to be made of a hard plastic or composite material, with a slightly textured surface for improved grip. The shaft is a solid, straight rod, colored red, and extends to a dark, cross-shaped Phillips tip suitable for driving screws with a corresponding head. The transition between the handle and shaft is marked by a black collar, likely for added durability and to prevent slippage. The overall construction suggests durability and suitability for both professional and household tasks. No visible branding or markings are present on the handle or shaft.
description: A red and tan contoured-handled Phillips screwdriver with a hard plastic grip and a straight red shaft ending in a cross-shaped tip. The tool is sturdy, ergonomic, and unmarked.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the screwdriver, highlighting the contoured handle and straight shaft with a visible Phillips tip.
description_view_1: End-on view of the handle, emphasizing its rounded, ergonomic shape and dual-tone coloration.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the handle's contour and the straight shaft with the Phillips tip.
description_view_3: View from the tip, showing the cross-shaped Phillips head and the circular end of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: contoured, dual-tone, hard plastic, ergonomic, grippy
